# AidiPanel

> A lightweight Nginx server control panel with native FastCGI Cache, per-site Linux-user and PHP-FPM isolation, and a focused web UI plus CLI.

## Current release

- Stable release: v1.2.0
- Website: https://aidipanel.com/
- Documentation: https://aidipanel.com/docs/
- Demo: https://demo.aidipanel.com/
- Source: https://github.com/rezzaidr/AidiPanel
- Releases: https://github.com/rezzaidr/AidiPanel/releases

## Installation

Fresh Ubuntu systems must be fully updated first:

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade -y
```

Reboot before continuing when `/var/run/reboot-required` exists. Install the latest stable release with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://get.aidipanel.com | sudo bash
```

For checksum verification and options, use the installation guide.

## Primary documentation

- [Introduction](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/introduction): Product scope, architecture principles, and current capabilities.
- [Installation](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/installation): Requirements, system preparation, verified installation, options, credentials, updates, and uninstall guidance.
- [Quick start](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/quick-start): Fresh server to an isolated first site.
- [Site management](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/sites): Site types, WordPress provisioning, PHP, Nginx, permissions, logs, and guarded deletion.
- [Site operations](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/operations): Files, databases, phpMyAdmin, cron, backups, jailed SFTP, security controls, accounts, 2FA, and diagnostics.
- [FastCGI Cache](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/fastcgi-cache): Page-cache rules, bypasses, purge behavior, zones, and WordPress integration.
- [SSL / TLS](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/ssl): Temporary self-signed certificates, Let's Encrypt, imports, renewal, force HTTPS, and HSTS.
- [Security model](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/security): Runtime isolation, privileged operations, firewall preservation, authentication, and update integrity.
- [Architecture](https://aidipanel.com/docs/guide/architecture): Services, users, sockets, request flow, and directory layout.
- [CLI reference](https://aidipanel.com/docs/reference/cli): User-facing CLI command groups, arguments, and safe examples.
- [Troubleshooting](https://aidipanel.com/docs/troubleshooting/): Symptom-based installation, HTTP, PHP, SSL, cache, permission, and database diagnostics.
- [Roadmap](https://aidipanel.com/docs/roadmap): Available, planned, and considered features.

## Important behavior

- The panel application and its PHP-FPM process run as the dedicated `aidipanel` account.
- Nginx runs as `www-data` and receives only the access needed to serve site files.
- Every PHP site has a dedicated no-login Linux user and PHP-FPM pool.
- WordPress provisioning requires title, admin username, admin password over stdin, and admin email; it creates WordPress and its database atomically.
- New sites initially allow HTTP and use a temporary self-signed certificate for HTTPS.
- Force HTTPS and HSTS are opt-in after a trusted certificate is active.
- UFW setup preserves existing rules and detected SSH ports.
- `aidipanel self:update` verifies release checksums and preserves panel data and credentials.
